safe_runtime_summary: Visiting clinical electives at UPMC are governed by the University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine (UPSOM); two parallel programs apply. The domestic program serves North-American LCME / AOA medical students in their fourth year via VSLO. The international program serves enrolled international medical students in their final year of medical education only — graduates are not eligible.

cost_wording: USD 4500 per clinical elective (international program); domestic fee not stated on source
application_wording: VSLO or paper via MSRIS@medschool.pitt.edu — paper preferred

safe_runtime_summary: Lincoln is one site within NYC Health + Hospitals; visiting medical-student access flows through the system-level MOSAIC Visiting Scholars Program. Eligibility is restricted to U.S. allopathic (LCME) or osteopathic (AOA) medical students who have completed core rotations. A four-week rotation includes shadowing, didactics, mentorship, and simulation; participants are matched to a NYC H+H participating site (Lincoln-specific placement is not separately enumerated). A specialty-specific Emergency Medicine MS3/4 rotation page also exists at the Lincoln Emergency Medicine residency site.
